label: OEF Opening Strikes — Kabul (Oct 7, 2001)
notes: US and British cruise missiles and aircraft strike Taliban targets in Kabul on the first night of Operation Enduring Freedom

label: OEF Opening Strikes — Kandahar
notes: Tomahawk cruise missiles from US Navy ships in the Arabian Sea target Taliban's Kandahar headquarters and training facilities

label: OEF Strikes — Jalalabad & Nangarhar
notes: US B-52 bombers and Navy aircraft strike al-Qaeda training camps in Nangarhar province surrounding Jalalabad

label: Northern Alliance Advance on Mazar-e-Sharif
notes: Northern Alliance forces, supported by US Special Forces, advance from their positions and capture Mazar-e-Sharif from Taliban forces

label: Northern Alliance Enters Kabul
notes: Northern Alliance forces advance from Panjshir Valley and Bagram to capture Kabul after overnight Taliban withdrawal

label: Bin Laden Tora Bora Escape Route (Dec 2001)
notes: Osama bin Laden's estimated escape route from Tora Bora cave complex into Pakistan's tribal areas during the battle; remains unconfirmed exactly which pass he used

label: Taliban IED Belt — Kandahar-Helmand Road
notes: Major IED campaign route along Highway 1 between Kandahar and Helmand; most dangerous stretch of road in Afghanistan for NATO forces 2007-2014

label: CIA Drone Operations — FATA
notes: CIA MQ-9 Reaper drone strike corridors targeting al-Qaeda and TTP leadership in North and South Waziristan from bases in Afghanistan and Gulf states

label: NATO Ground Supply Route — Pakistan
notes: Primary NATO/US ground logistics route from Karachi port through Pakistan to Khyber Pass and into Afghanistan; Pakistan closed route Nov 2011-July 2012 after Salala checkpoint attack

label: Northern Distribution Network (NDN)
notes: Alternative NATO supply route through Central Asia (Latvia/Baltic → Russia/Central Asia → Uzbekistan/Tajikistan) activated to bypass Pakistan after Khyber Pass closure

label: Haqqani Network Kabul Attack Corridors
notes: Haqqani Network's operational pipeline from Khost/Paktia into Kabul for conducting complex attacks including the 2008 Indian Embassy bombing and 2011 US Embassy attack

label: Quetta Shura → Kandahar Command Route
notes: Taliban leadership command-and-control route from Quetta (Pakistan) into Kandahar province; used for directing southern insurgency operations throughout conflict

label: Quetta Shura → Helmand Insurgency Route
notes: Taliban reinforcement and command route from Pakistani sanctuary in Quetta to Helmand province for insurgency operations against NATO forces

label: TTP Cross-Border Infiltration — Waziristan to Paktika
notes: TTP fighter movement from North Waziristan sanctuaries in Pakistan into Paktika and Paktia provinces of Afghanistan; used for regrouping and launching attacks into Pakistan

label: Pakistan Airstrikes — Paktika (Mar 2024)
notes: Pakistani Air Force F-16/JF-17 strikes targeting TTP camps in Paktika province; Taliban fired artillery in retaliation across border at Pakistani checkpoints

label: ISIS-K Expansion — Nangarhar to Kabul
notes: ISIS-K expansion of attack operations from Nangarhar stronghold into Kabul and other provinces; attack cells established using infiltrated operatives through commercial traffic

label: Taliban 2021 Offensive — Southern Thrust
notes: Taliban's main offensive thrust in July-August 2021 from Pakistan-Afghanistan border region into Kandahar and Helmand provinces, capturing provincial capitals in days

label: Taliban 2021 Offensive — Northern Drive
notes: Taliban's northern offensive capturing Mazar-e-Sharif and Kunduz in rapid succession; last-ditch Afghan resistance at Mazar collapsed August 14, 2021

label: Taliban Final Advance on Kabul
notes: Taliban forces encircled Kabul from multiple directions on Aug 14-15, 2021; city fell without major fighting after Ghani fled; Taliban entered Kabul on afternoon of August 15

label: US Evacuation Airlift — Kabul to Gulf
notes: C-17 and C-130 evacuation flights from Kabul airport to Al Udeid Air Base (Qatar) and other Gulf staging areas during chaotic two-week evacuation of 124,000+ people

label: CIA Drone Strike — Zawahiri, Kabul (Jul 2022)
notes: CIA over-the-horizon Hellfire R9X 'flying ginsu' drone strike from Gulf-based platform targeting Ayman al-Zawahiri's Sherpur neighborhood safehouse in Kabul

label: Doha Negotiations — Taliban-US Shuttle
notes: Diplomatic transit between Doha, Qatar (Taliban political office) and Kabul/Washington for Doha Agreement negotiations 2018-2020 and subsequent diplomatic engagement

label: Helmand Opium → Iran Drug Route
notes: Primary Afghan opium and heroin smuggling route from Helmand province through Nimroz into Iran and onward to European markets; Taliban taxed this route generating billions annually

label: Helmand Opium → Pakistan Drug Route
notes: Afghan heroin/opium trafficking route from Helmand and Kandahar through Baluchistan into Pakistan and then to global markets; finances Taliban and criminal networks

label: ISIS-K External Operations — Crocus City Hall
notes: ISIS-K recruited Tajik nationals in Moscow for the March 22, 2024 Crocus City Hall massacre (140+ killed), demonstrating external operations capability from Afghanistan-based leadership

label: TTP Attack Network — Lahore Operations
notes: TTP operational pipeline for attacks on Pakistani cities including the March 2009 attack on Sri Lanka cricket team bus and 2010 Data Darbar shrine bombing in Lahore

label: TTP North Waziristan → Peshawar Attacks
notes: TTP operational route from Waziristan into Peshawar for conducting suicide bombings; includes Jan 2023 mosque bombing (100+ dead) and Army Public School massacre (Dec 2014, 132 children killed)

label: APS School Massacre Route (Dec 16, 2014)
notes: TTP attack on Army Public School Peshawar killing 132 children and 17 staff; deadliest terrorist attack in Pakistan's history. Attackers infiltrated from Waziristan area.

label: ISI-Haqqani Network Liaison
notes: Alleged ISI support/coordination with Haqqani Network through handler network in Islamabad and North Waziristan; US intelligence documented links; Pakistan denied command relationship

label: ISIS-K Nangarhar ↔ Kunar Corridor
notes: ISIS-K main operational corridor linking Nangarhar and Kunar provinces along mountain terrain; used for fighter movement, resupply and command; difficult for both Taliban and coalition to disrupt

label: Afghan Refugee Flow — Torkham to Karachi
notes: Mass movement of Afghan refugees through Torkham border crossing and onward through Pakistan after Taliban takeover; Pakistan deported ~1.7 million undocumented Afghans in 2023-2024

label: Mansour Drone Strike — Baluchistan (May 2016)
notes: US RQ-9 Reaper drone strike in Pakistan's Baluchistan province killing Taliban leader Mullah Akhtar Mansour as he returned from Iran — first acknowledged strike outside FATA

label: B-52/B-1 Strike Sorties — Bagram to Helmand
notes: B-52 Stratofortress and B-1B Lancer bombers based at Bagram conducting close air support and interdiction missions over Helmand during NATO's most intense fighting 2006-2012

label: RPAS/Drone Operations — Kabul to Kandahar
notes: MQ-1 Predator and MQ-9 Reaper drone ISR and strike missions over Kandahar province; US expanded drone fleet from 40 aircraft in 2001 to over 7,000 by 2013

label: Iran Arms Supply to Taliban (Alleged)
notes: US military and ISAF reported Iranian arms including IED components and weapons reaching Taliban in Helmand via Iranian border in Nimroz and Farah; Iran denied orchestrated supply

label: China-Pakistan Economic Corridor (CPEC)
notes: China's $62 billion Belt and Road infrastructure investment linking Xinjiang through Pakistan to Gwadar port; TTP attacks on Chinese workers threaten CPEC security and China-Pakistan relations

label: US SOF Night Raids — Kabul to RC-East
notes: US Special Operations Forces (JSOC/DEVGRU) helicopter-borne night raid operations from Bagram/Kabul into Paktia/Paktika/Khost targeting HVTs; Afghans protested civilian casualties from raids

label: China-Taliban Diplomatic Engagement
notes: China maintains ambassador in Kabul under Taliban rule and has signed mineral extraction agreements. China's engagement motivated by ISIS-K threat to Xinjiang and CPEC security concerns.

label: ISIS-K External Plots — Europe
notes: Disrupted ISIS-K plots in Austria (Vienna concert attack thwarted Aug 2024) and Germany; attackers recruited via online networks traced to Afghanistan-based ISIS-K leadership

label: Russia-Taliban Arms (Alleged)
notes: US commander Gen. Nicholson and NATO reported intelligence suggesting Russia provided weapons to Taliban; Russia denied; Cold War-style Russian engagement used as leverage against US

label: Taliban-Central Asia Border — Amu Darya
notes: Taliban-Uzbekistan/Tajikistan border along Amu Darya River. Taliban occasionally clash with Tajik border forces. Tajikistan hosts Afghan resistance figures. Uzbekistan engages Taliban commercially.

label: Pakistan Artillery — Kunar (Sarkano/Narai, Mar 25)
damage: Residential areas and farmland in Sarkano and Narai districts struck; 2 drone strikes also conducted on Sarkano district center
casualties: 2 civilians killed, 8 wounded (Pajhwok Afghan News confirmed); 1 civilian killed at Sarkano district center
notes: Pakistan fired 85 artillery shells into Kunar province shortly after Eid ceasefire expired. Shells also struck Manura (2), Shultan (15), and Asadabad surroundings (5).
platform: Pakistani Army M109 / BM-21 artillery, PAF drones

label: Pakistan Op Ghazab-il-Haq — Paktika Strikes
damage: Pakistani forces struck alleged TTP/Taliban positions in Paktika province
notes: Operation Ghazab-il-Haq formally resumed March 26; strikes continued across Paktika, Khost, Kunar provinces. Pakistan also alleged to have fenced 32 sq km of Af-Pak border territory.
platform: PAF F-16 / JF-17, Pakistani Army artillery

label: Afghan Sniper Attack — Torkham (Mar 27)
damage: Frontier Corps post at Awal Khan, Torkham
casualties: 1 FC Subedar wounded; 2 personnel injured total
notes: Afghan sniper shot Pakistani Frontier Corps Subedar at Awal Khan border post near Torkham at ~9:30 PM. Led to immediate Torkham closure and suspension of Afghan deportations.

label: PAF Strikes 41 Locations — Nangarhar & Kunar (Mar 30)
damage: Brigade and battalion HQs in Nangarhar struck; army HQ in Kandahar targeted; residential areas in Kunar hit
casualties: 1 killed, 16 wounded (predominantly women and children) — Afghanistan deputy spokesperson Fitrat
notes: PAF conducted the widest single-day strike package of the conflict to date, hitting 41 locations across Nangarhar, Kunar, and Kandahar provinces. Pakistan denied targeting civilians, claiming strikes were limited to Taliban military infrastructure.
platform: PAF F-16 / JF-17 Thunder, armed drones

label: Pakistan Artillery — Kunar-Bajaur Border (Mar 30)
damage: Residential areas along Kunar-Bajaur border area struck
casualties: Multiple civilian casualties reported alongside the PAF airstrikes
notes: Heavy artillery fire exchanged along the Kunar province-Bajaur district border following PAF airstrikes. Afghan forces returned fire across the Durand Line.

label: Pakistan Mortar/Rocket/Drone Strikes — Kunar, Paktika, Khost (Apr 2)
damage: Sarkano and Manogai districts in Kunar, Shkin (Paktika), Zazi Maidan (Khost) struck
notes: Pakistan launched sustained mortar, rocket, and drone strikes across three eastern Afghan provinces. Taliban deputy spokesman Fitrat confirmed continuous attacks. Strikes continued simultaneously with China-mediated peace talks in Urumqi.
platform: Pakistani Army artillery, PAF armed drones

label: Pakistan Artillery — Kunar (185 Shells, Apr 3-4)
damage: Single Kunar district sustained barrage; 178 additional shells struck elsewhere in province
notes: Pakistan fired 185 long-range artillery shells into a single district in Kunar on April 3-4, plus 178 additional shells in other Kunar areas. Most intensive sustained bombardment of a single province since conflict began.

label: Taliban Ground Assault — Ghulam Khan Border Post (Apr 6)
damage: Pakistani border post in Ghulam Khan Sector, North Waziristan
casualties: 37 attackers killed, 80+ wounded; 0 Pakistani casualties reported
notes: Coordinated Taliban and Fitna al-Khawarij (TTP) assault on Pakistani border post repelled by security forces. Largest single ground engagement of the conflict. Signals Taliban shift from passive defense to active cross-border assaults.

label: TTP Infiltration Attempt — North Waziristan (Apr 25-27)
notes: Large TTP column attempted to cross from Afghanistan into North Waziristan district of KPK. Pakistani security forces engaged the infiltrators; 71 TTP militants killed in heavy clashes spanning April 25-27. Largest TTP cross-border infiltration attempt since the Urumqi non-escalation framework took hold. Confirms TTP's continued use of Afghan territory as staging area despite inter-state non-escalation.
platform: TTP fighters with small arms; Pakistan Army / Frontier Corps response

label: Pakistan Artillery — Asadabad, Kunar Capital (Apr 8)
damage: Asadabad city center, capital of Kunar province
notes: Pakistan struck Asadabad, Kunar's provincial capital, on the same day China announced Afghanistan and Pakistan agreed to explore a 'comprehensive solution.' The shelling of a provincial capital while Urumqi peace talks continued drew Afghan condemnation.
